title: Hastingsia alba
taxonomy: Hastingsia alba is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Liliaceae, commonly known as the lily family. It belongs to the genus Hastingsia, which is a small group of plants native to the western coast of North America. The family Liliaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes various types of lilies, orchids, and other related species. Hastingsia alba is a rare and endemic species found in certain regions of California, USA. The classification of Hastingsia alba reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Liliaceae family.

intro: Hastingsia alba, also known as white hastingsia or white false camas, is a perennial plant species native to the coastal regions of California and Oregon in the United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Hastingsia alba include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Hastingsia alba is commonly prepared as a tincture or tea, and is also used in topical ointments to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
